nodes.name: Sticky Note2

parameters.content:
# Telegram N8N workflow (de)activator

## What does it do?
This workflow helps you to quickly activate or deactivate a workflow through Telegram. Sometimes we are not able to access a PC to resolve an issue if something goes wrong with a workflow. If you, like me, use Telegram to send yourself error reports, you can quickly react in case of urgency. Just by sending '/stop' combined with the name you use for a workflow, you can deactivate a workflow, or reactivate it with '/start'. For example '/stop marketing'.

Walkthrough: {{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }} (6min)

## Instructions
1. Create a Telegram API key through botfather ({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }} Add it to the telegram credentials.
2. For the N8N nodes, go to settings in your n8n instance. Then 'n8n API' and 'create an API key'. 
3. To ensure that only we can send commands to the bot, we need the chat ID of our DM with our newly created bot. Open the the Telegram trigger and click on 'listen to events'.
4. Go to Telegram and send a direct message to the bot, this will trigger the Telegram node.
5. Go to the filter node and fill in the chat id you want to filter for with the data you got from the test event in the Telegram node.
6. In the first Switch node you can find the commands, in this case it is '/start' and '/stop'. When you send a message to your bot starting with either of those, it will go to the next switch nodes.
7. Next it will check what other word it contains. As an example I have used the words 'marketing' and 'sales', both corresponding to a marketing and sales workflow. 
8. The last nodes will either activate or deactivate a workflow.

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

### Switch depending on content

# Telegram N8N workflow (de)activator

## What does it do?
This workflow helps you to quickly activate or deactivate a workflow through Telegram. Sometimes we are not able to access a PC to resolve an issue if something goes wrong with a workflow. If you, like me, use Telegram to send yourself error reports, you can quickly react in case of urgency. Just by sending '/stop' combined with the name you use for a workflow, you can deactivate a workflow, or reactivate it with '/start'. For example '/stop marketing'.

Walkthrough: {{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }} (6min)

## Instructions
1. Create a Telegram API key through botfather ({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }} Add it to the telegram credentials.
2. For the N8N nodes, go to settings in your n8n instance. Then 'n8n API' and 'create an API key'. 
3. To ensure that only we can send commands to the bot, we need the chat ID of our DM with our newly created bot. Open the the Telegram trigger and click on 'listen to events'.
4. Go to Telegram and send a direct message to the bot, this will trigger the Telegram node.
5. Go to the filter node and fill in the chat id you want to filter for with the data you got from the test event in the Telegram node.
6. In the first Switch node you can find the commands, in this case it is '/start' and '/stop'. When you send a message to your bot starting with either of those, it will go to the next switch nodes.
7. Next it will check what other word it contains. As an example I have used the words 'marketing' and 'sales', both corresponding to a marketing and sales workflow. 
8. The last nodes will either activate or deactivate a workflow.

prerequisites: An active n8n instance installed locally, in a Docker container, or hosted on n8n Cloud.
Basic familiarity with importing JSON templates in n8n.
setupInstructions: Download the JSON configuration for this workflow.
Ensure you set up credentials for all connected integrations.
Test your trigger and run execution.
configurationSteps: Verify the settings of the trigger node "Receive commands from Telegram".
Open the "Sticky Note" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Keep only messages from a specific chat id" node (n8n-nodes-base.filter) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note1" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Deactivate the marketing workflow" node (n8n-nodes-base.n8n) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Deactivate the sales workflow" node (n8n-nodes-base.n8n) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Activate the marketing workflow" node (n8n-nodes-base.n8n) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Switch depending on content (activate)" node (n8n-nodes-base.switch) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note2" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Activate the sales workflow" node (n8n-nodes-base.n8n) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Switch depending on command" node (n8n-nodes-base.switch) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Switch depending on content (deactivate)" node (n8n-nodes-base.switch) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Error Handler" node (n8n-nodes-base.stopAndError) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
No global variables node (Set node) is present. If you need dynamic constants, add a Set node after the trigger.
testingSteps: Click the "Test step" or "Listen for test event" button on your Trigger node.
Send a test trigger from your source service (e.g. fill a test form or trigger a webhook trigger).
Verify that the node canvas lights up green, indicating successful data flow.
Open the final destination node to confirm that output records were saved correctly.
instructions.expectedResult: Upon trigger activation, inputs will flow through the configured nodes and execute actions successfully.
troubleshooting: Check credential warning symbols. Most workflow issues stem from expired OAuth connections or missing API permissions.
Verify that the data keys coming from your Trigger node match the exact names mapped in subsequent nodes (e.g., {{ $json.body.email }}).
Open the Execution history tab in n8n to view logs and determine which node failed.
securityNotes: Never hardcode API keys, passwords, or authentication bearer tokens in the plain text fields of node parameters.
If you download or share this workflow JSON, sanitize any sensitive parameters or configuration nodes.
customizationIdeas: Add an email node at the end to notify you of successful executions.
Incorporate an AI LLM node to summarize trigger payloads before writing them to integrations.
